content.problemStatement:
Aujasvit just came up with a new game to play with his friends. NNN people stand in a circle, each assigned an index from 111 to NNN in clockwise order. Then the following operation is done N−1N-1N−1 times.


The person with **the lowest index** holds a coin.
Then, the coin then moves to the next person clockwise M−1M-1M−1 times.
Then, the person who is holding the coin is removed from the circle.
The last person remaining after N−1N-1N−1 operations is the winner.

Aujasvit has already decided on the integer MMM to be used in the game. Before inviting people to play his game, Aujasvit wants to know the winner if the game has 111 player, 222 players, 333 players, ..., XXX players. That is, you need to output the winner when N=1,N=2,...,N=XN = 1, N = 2, ..., N = XN=1,N=2,...,N=X.


Input Format

The first line of each input contains TTT - the number of test cases. The test cases then follow.
The only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers MMM and XXX.


Output Format
For each testcase, output XXX integers A1,A2,…,AXA_1, A_2, \dots, A_XA1​,A2​,…,AX​, where AiA_iAi​ is the index of the winner if iii people are playing this game.
constraints: Sum of XXX over all testcases is not more than 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

When there is only 111 player, they are the winner. Therefore player 111 wins.
When there are 222 players, player 222 is removed in the first round. Therefore player 111 wins.
When there are 333 players, player 222 is removed in the first round, and player 333 is removed in the second round. Therefore player 111 wins.
